classifications.phrase: materially higher than expected at the time of the August Statement
Headline inflation rose sharply to 3.2 per cent over the year
underlying inflation rising above 3 per cent in coming quarters
labour market conditions remain a little tight
growth in unit labour costs remains high
pick-up in private demand continues to exceed expectations, this could increase the demand for labour,…
some inflationary pressure may remain in the economy
recent evidence of more persistent inflation

payload.rationale: The RBA signals a hawkish stance by emphasizing persistent and higher-than-expected inflation, tight labour market conditions, and elevated unit labour costs, justifying a pause in rate cuts despite recent easing.
